# UC-14.1.32 · Sub-Meter Energy Distribution by System

## Description

Sub-metering breaks total energy into HVAC, lighting, plug loads, and other systems. Knowing which system consumes the most energy directs efficiency investments where they'll have the biggest impact — typically HVAC at 40-60%.

## Value

Sub-metering breaks total energy into HVAC, lighting, plug loads, and other systems. Knowing which system consumes the most energy directs efficiency investments where they'll have the biggest impact — typically HVAC at 40-60%.

## Implementation

Deploy sub-meters on HVAC, lighting, plug load, and elevator panels. Ingest readings. Calculate percentage distribution. Trend over time. Identify systems with growing share.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Smart sub-meters via Modbus TCP, BMS energy management module.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=bms:energy`, `sourcetype=bms:meter`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy sub-meters on HVAC, lighting, plug load, and elevator panels. Ingest readings. Calculate percentage distribution. Trend over time. Identify systems with growing share.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=building sourcetype="bms:energy" meter_type="sub_meter"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(kwh_consumed) as daily_kwh by building_name, system_type, _time
| eventstats sum(daily_kwh) as total_kwh by building_name, _time
| eval pct_of_total=round(daily_kwh*100/total_kwh, 1)
| table _time, building_name, system_type, daily_kwh, pct_of_total
| sort _time, building_name, -pct_of_total
```

Understanding this SPL

**Sub-Meter Energy Distribution by System** — Sub-metering breaks total energy into HVAC, lighting, plug loads, and other systems. Knowing which system consumes the most energy directs efficiency investments where they'll have the biggest impact — typically HVAC at 40-60%.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=bms:energy`, `sourcetype=bms:meter`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Smart sub-meters via Modbus TCP, BMS energy management module. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: building; **sourcetype**: bms:energy. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=building, sourcetype="bms:energy". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by building_name, system_type, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by building_name, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pct_of_total** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Sub-Meter Energy Distribution by System**): table _time, building_name, system_type, daily_kwh, pct_of_total
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search in a known window. Spot-check a few events against the BMS console (for example Niagara, Tridium, or Honeywell) and confirm zones, values, and alarms line up. Verify field names and that the right roles can search the index.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie chart of energy by system; stacked area chart over time; system comparison across buildings.

## SPL

```spl
index=building sourcetype="bms:energy" meter_type="sub_meter"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(kwh_consumed) as daily_kwh by building_name, system_type, _time
| eventstats sum(daily_kwh) as total_kwh by building_name, _time
| eval pct_of_total=round(daily_kwh*100/total_kwh, 1)
| table _time, building_name, system_type, daily_kwh, pct_of_total
| sort _time, building_name, -pct_of_total
```

## Visualization

Pie chart of energy by system; stacked area chart over time; system comparison across buildings.
